title: senecio congestus subsp. palustris
intro: Senecio congestus subsp. palustris, also known as marsh ragwort, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and is native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Senecio congestus subsp. palustris are derived from its leaves, roots, and flowers, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is used for its health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Senecio congestus subsp. palustris can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used in traditional medicine to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
